page: Amazon Web Services r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics generally fall within the average range. Memory performance shows a distinct tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 568.6 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Computational tasks such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and static web serving demonstrate stable, average-tier throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.2xlarge offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local storage or massive uncached memory throughput. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, medium-scale web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores at 3.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth remains a relative bottleneck. This resource profile delivers a cost-effective balance for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high local disk I/O. It is primarily suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 64 GB of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory (8 GB per core)
Delivers strong single-core CPU benchmark performance
Features a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
